channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The public health team watching the World Cup
item.description: As millions gather for the World Cup, a scrappy public health operation led by a team at Georgetown is tracking disease threats using surveys, wastewater and online chatter to spot outbreaks early.
item.content:encoded: As millions gather for the World Cup, a scrappy public health operation led by a team at Georgetown is tracking disease threats using surveys, wastewater and online chatter to spot outbreaks early.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Recent studies show fathers' brains change after bringing home a new baby
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to Washington Post journalist Richard Sima about how fathers' brains change after bringing home a new baby.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to Washington Post journalist Richard Sima about how fathers' brains change after bringing home a new baby.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: A new survey on dads found that 9 out of 10 had a surprising reaction to fatherhood
item.description: Men are traditionally thought of as providers for their children. But a report that interviewed thousands of fathers found them embracing another role.
item.content:encoded:
Men are traditionally thought of as providers for their children. But a report that interviewed thousands of fathers found them embracing another role.

(Image credit: From left: family photo; family photo; family photo)
item.dc:creator: Kamala Thiagarajan

item.title: Want to improve your agility? Try these exercises that combine speed and strength
item.description: These simple movements combine speed and strength to train your body's ability to rapidly generate force. They can also help prevent injury and boost agility.
item.content:encoded:
These simple movements combine speed and strength to train your body's ability to rapidly generate force. They can also help prevent injury and boost agility.

(Image credit: Margaret Cirino)
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Flu outbreak tests new Pentagon vaccine policy
item.description: A flu outbreak at a Texas Air Force Base is fueling debate over the Pentagon's decision to end the military's flu vaccine mandate. Former military health official Jose Sanchez explains the stakes.
item.content:encoded: A flu outbreak at a Texas Air Force Base is fueling debate over the Pentagon's decision to end the military's flu vaccine mandate. Former military health official Jose Sanchez explains the stakes.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: DOJ memo stokes fear among disability advocates of a return to institutionalization
item.description: The Justice Department's opinion challenges civil rights protections that have long treated the institutionalization of disabled Americans as a last resort.
item.content:encoded:
The Justice Department's opinion challenges civil rights protections that have long treated the institutionalization of disabled Americans as a last resort.

(Image credit: Patrick Semansky)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: A top pulmonologist reviews advancements in the 'War on Cancer' over the past 50 years
item.description: NPR's Scott Simon speaks with Dr. Robert A. Winn about the progress the United States has made in treating cancer.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Simon speaks with Dr. Robert A. Winn about the progress the United States has made in treating cancer.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Benk

item.title: These nuns spent a lifetime helping others. In their last years, who will help them?
item.description: The sisters of Uganda are teachers, health-care advocates and more. Those who are in their twilight of their life need help. Who will come to their aid?
item.content:encoded:
The sisters of Uganda are teachers, health-care advocates and more. Those who are in their twilight of their life need help. Who will come to their aid?

(Image credit: Stuart Tibaweswa for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sophie Neiman

item.title: Tick season is getting worse. Can managing deer help?
item.description: Health officials and researchers hope that efforts to control deer populations, which serve as "party buses" for mating ticks, can reverse the tide of ticks and the illnesses they cause.
item.content:encoded:
Health officials and researchers hope that efforts to control deer populations, which serve as "party buses" for mating ticks, can reverse the tide of ticks and the illnesses they cause.

(Image credit: Mary Swift/iStockphoto)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: 'Toy Story 5' gets at something very real: It's hard to keep kids off screens in summer
item.description: Kids' screen use goes way up in the summertime. And just as the movie Toy Story 5 portrays, that can be problematic for children. Here are tips for parents to help their kids manage screens and have fun IRL this summer.
item.content:encoded:
Toy Story 5. It opens in theaters on Friday, June 19.'/>

Kids' screen use goes way up in the summertime. And just as the movie Toy Story 5 portrays, that can be problematic for children. Here are tips for parents to help their kids manage screens and have fun IRL this summer.

(Image credit: Disney/Pixar)
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee
